lines.en_lit: that in accordance with neither Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov nor Rabbi Yehuda,
MISHNA: daily offering is sacrificed by nine by ten by eleven, by twelve no fewer and no more How so? itself by nine On the festival in hand a jug of water there are ten

In the afternoon eleven itself by nine, and two in whose hands two logs On Shabbat, eleven itself by nine, and two in whose hands two vessels of frankincense the shewbread. And on Shabbat that within the festival in whose hand jug of water

GEMARA: Rabbi Abba, and some say Rami bar Ḥama, and some say Rabbi Yoḥanan, said: On the festival they pour the water libation only the daily morning offering From where from that it is taught And on Shabbat that within the festival in whose hand jug of water, And if it should enter your mind they pour during the daily afternoon offering so, you find a weekday as well,
Rav Ashi said: We too have learned And they would say to the pourer: Raise your hand because one time poured onto his feet, all the people pelted him with their etrogim learn from this

It was taught Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai says: From where that the daily afternoon offering requires two logs by two priests? As it is stated and lay out wood etzim If this is not applicable to the daily morning offering, as it is written about “And the priest shall burn wood on it every morning, and arrange on it” apply it to the daily afternoon offering.
But say this and this, the daily morning offering, and the Merciful One states Perform and then return and perform If so, should say: “And he shall burn “And he shall burn
If the Merciful One had written “And he shall burn I would have said one not two. it teaches us one should perform and two should perform

If so, the verse should say: “And he shall burn And they shall burn Or, alternatively, And he shall lay out “And they shall lay out What “And he shall burn and then: “And they shall lay out Learn from this as we have said,
Rabbi Ḥiyya taught: Sometimes thirteen sometimes fourteen sometimes fifteen sometimes sixteen
That not in accordance with Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov but is in accordance with Rabbi Yehuda.

MISHNA: A ram is sacrificed by eleven The flesh by five The intestines, and the fine flour and the wine two each, A bull sacrificed by twenty-four The head and the leg the head by one and the leg by two. The tail and the leg The tail by two and the leg by two. The breast and the neck The breast by one and the neck by three two forelegs by two and the two flanks by two. The intestines and the fine flour and the wine by three each,
In what is this statement said, in communal offerings. However, in an individual offering if wishes to sacrifice he sacrifice the flaying and the cutting of these, those, they are equal,
GEMARA: taught equal their flaying and cutting, by a non-priest.

Ḥizkiya said: From where equal their flaying and cutting? by a non-priest? it is stated: “The sons of Aaron the priest shall place fire on the altar” placing fire requires priesthood, flaying and cutting do not require priesthood.
